title: Adapt Presentation without Removing Useful Feedback
summary: Build and verify css user preferences in shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain css user preferences from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply css user preferences without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

options.text: Shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review case 1: preference media queries should disable all useful feedback and state changes.

Shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review case 1: set display none on animated status messages under reduced motion.

steps.title: 02 · Read the shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves css user preferences when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review.
steps.why: Adapt Presentation without Removing Useful Feedback makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review.

content.text: Preference media features include reduced motion, increased contrast, color scheme, forced colors, and in some environments reduced transparency. Adaptations should reduce unwanted effects while preserving state, hierarchy, and feedback. Reduced motion does not mean hiding focus or instant unexplained state changes, and dark mode is not automatic inversion. Apply it to shift deck motion contrast and color-scheme preference review.
